opp.name: Farplas_AS_2026
opp.next_step: Demo scheduled with Farplas and EY team on 9th July

items.quote: Demo scheduled with Farplas and EY team on 9th July

metrics.narrative: No business problem or quantified impact is captured anywhere: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c and Compelling_Event__c are all empty, and no buyer call exists to read the pain from. The deal is one day old and was auto-progressed to Qualified without discovery content on record. The next move is to use the 9 July demo to surface and quantify the procurement pain (cycle time, spend under management, contract leakage) directly with Farplas.

sources: SF account contacts: Ali Guersoy, Burak Birdal (Digital Transformation)
champion.narrative: No champion is identified. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is empty and there are zero contact roles on the opportunity, so single-thread risk is at its maximum: there is currently no named individual advocating for Zycus. Account-level Digital Transformation contacts (Ali Guersoy, Burak Birdal) are logical champion candidates given the digital-transformation framing, but none is confirmed on this deal. Securing a named champion at or before the 9 July demo is the highest-priority relationship move.

competition.narrative: The competitive landscape is unknown. Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c and Replacing_What__c are all empty, and no call has surfaced who else Farplas is evaluating or what the incumbent is. EY's involvement is a wildcard: if EY has an incumbent relationship or has already recommended a competitor, that is a live threat; if EY is a neutral advisor, it is neutral. Until the field is known, no wedge or differentiation can be positioned. Mapping the competitive field is a ranked discovery move for the 9 July demo.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific pain is articulated anywhere. Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c and What_if_this_is_not_done__c are empty and there is no call to quote. The product scope (iContract, iSource, eProcurement) implies Farplas is looking at sourcing, contract management and procurement automation, but that is an inference from the products selected, not confirmed pain. Confirming the operational pain owner and their specific problem at the 9 July demo is the first real qualification step.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are on record: Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty and there is no MSA, SOW, InfoSec, or procurement-channel detail. The deal is too early for paper, but EY's potential role as an implementer could shape the eventual scoping and contracting path. Visibility will come once the evaluation structure is understood after the 9 July demo.

sources: SF account contacts: Arda Denizhan (CFO), Ali Inamlik (CFO)
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unmapped. X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are all empty/false, and there are no contact roles on the opportunity. Account-level contacts include a CFO and Executive Board Member, Arda Denizhan, and a CFO, Ali Inamlik, as plausible budget authorities, but neither is confirmed on this opportunity by any call or email. Identifying and gaining access to the budget owner is a ranked move.

sources: SF Task 'Get clarity on the evaluation stages and influence the process' 2026-06-29
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown: What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Multiple_approvals__c and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all empty. The strongest process clue is that EY is joining the 9 July demo, which signals an advisor or systems integrator may be steering or scoping the evaluation. Clarifying EY's mandate (advisor, evaluator, or future implementer) is essential, as it shapes who decides and how. An open Task already exists to get clarity on the evaluation stages and influence the process.

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria are documented. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c (false) carry nothing, and no demo or workshop has occurred to read criteria from. The 9 July demo with Farplas and EY is the first opportunity to capture what they will weigh, especially since EY's presence suggests a structured evaluation framework. Extracting the criteria at that demo is the priority.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Just qualified with no champion, no EB, every MEDDPICC element a gap and no AI positioning done yet
A demo with Farplas and the EY team is scheduled for 9 Jul
No meetings yet, no contact roles, and the competitive landscape is unmapped

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Competitive landscape is completely unmapped.

bullets.text: No champion, no discovery, unknown competition, early stage

blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

Clarify EY's role in the evaluation at or before the 9 July demo. (by 9 Jul)
Identify a Farplas champion and the budget owner, then add buyer contact roles to the opportunity. (by 14 Jul)

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c and AIS_Why__c are all empty and no call exists to assess AI appetite. Tier is unknown; defaulting to AI Curious pending the 9 July demo.

items.change: Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified
Owner changed from Krina Shah to Caroline Lacocque

deal_movement.summary: The opportunity was created 2026-06-29 and moved Initial Interest to Qualified the same day, with the close date pushed from 2026-12-29 to 2027-01-31 and ownership transferred from Krina Shah to Caroline Lacocque. There has been no buyer-facing activity since; the only events on record are these record-setup changes and a batch of auto-generated Opportunity Health tasks. Per the authoritative pulse the deal is DARK.

items.detail: Zero buyer-side contact roles and no champion identified. Single-thread risk is very high. If the single contact disengages or is not the decision-maker, the deal dies.

Economic Buyer unmapped. No visibility into who controls the budget or approves the commercial decision. Without EB access, pricing and commercial conversations cannot advance, and forecast defensibility is weak.

Competitive landscape unknown. No insight into who else Farplas is evaluating, what the incumbent is, or how vendors will be compared. Cannot position differentiation or wedge strategy without knowing the field.

EY's role unclear. Next Step mentions 'Demo scheduled with Farplas and EY team on 9th July.' If EY is steering the evaluation or scoping implementation, Zycus must understand their mandate and influence. If EY has already recommended a competitor or has an incumbent relationship, that is a competitive threat.
EY's role in the 9 July demo is undefined and could be an advisor, evaluator, or competitor vector.
No economic buyer or champion mapped; deal is single-threaded into zero contacts.

confidence_signals.summary: No buyer engagement signal exists; the deal is dark per the authoritative pulse. The 9 July demo is the only forward catalyst.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-29 (~1 day old), moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 2026-06-29. Close date 2027-01-31 is 215 days out. Qualified_Submission_Date 2026-06-29. No buyer call, no LastActivityDate, zero contact roles. Per the authoritative pulse the deal is DARK: no verified buyer engagement and no logged rep outreach. Forecast Pipeline is honest for this state.
evidence: No buyer calls found across opp, account, and five buyer attendee-email Avoma pulls (Avoma discovery 2026-06-30).
Next_Step__c: demo with Farplas and EY scheduled 9 July 2026 (SF Next_Step__c).
north_star_verdict.headline: Brand-new Qualified deal with zero buyer engagement on record. The only forward signal is a Farplas plus EY demo set for 9 July 2026 (in ~9 days); win or lose, the deal hinges on that meeting landing.

flags: Single-thread risk extreme: zero contact roles on the opportunity, no named buyer contacts, no champion, no multi-threading. If the 9 July demo attendee who scheduled the meeting is not the decision-maker or disengages post-demo, the deal dies. Multi-threading must begin at the demo by identifying all attendees, mapping their roles, and securing introductions to the Economic Buyer and additional stakeholders.

Economic Buyer unmapped: no visibility into who controls the 150k budget or approves vendor selection. Without EB access, pricing and commercial conversations cannot progress, and forecast is indefensible. The 9 July demo attendee list will reveal whether a senior finance or procurement executive is present; if not, securing EB access is the immediate post-demo action.

Competitive landscape unknown: no competitor documented in Salesforce or Avoma, no insight into who else Farplas is evaluating or what the current-state solution is. Cannot position differentiation or wedge strategy without knowing the field. The 9 July demo is the moment to ask directly who else they are looking at and what they have learned from other demos.

No quantified business case: no documented pain, metrics, or ROI. Without a quantified value case anchored to a pain owner, the deal cannot progress to Economic Buyer sponsorship or proposal. Discovery at the 9 July demo must extract the business problem, the operational or financial cost of the current state, and the quantified outcome Farplas is targeting.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a one-day-old Qualified deal that is dark on buyer engagement. The win strategy is entirely about converting the 9 July demo into real discovery, multi-threading from zero, and decoding EY's role.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is completely unmapped. No competitor documented in Salesforce (Competitors__c null per ground truth), no free-text field mentions a rival or incumbent, and no Avoma calls exist to surface competitive intel. The 9 July demo is the first opportunity to extract who else Farplas is evaluating, what the current-state solution is (manual processes, legacy ERP procurement, incumbent vendor), and whether EY has a preferred vendor shortlist. EY's advisory role is a structural uncertainty: if EY has recommended a competitor in prior engagements or has deeper relationships with SAP/Coupa/Ivalua, Zycus enters at a disadvantage. Without knowing the competition, differentiation and wedge strategy are impossible.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: No AI/Merlin positioning has occurred and no AIS score is set. Merlin_Products__c is empty. The 9 July demo is the first chance to land an AI narrative if Farplas signals appetite.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No customer expectations or evaluation criteria are on record. Product scope (iContract, iSource, eProcurement) indicates a source-to-contract plus procurement interest, but fit cannot be assessed until criteria are surfaced at the 9 July demo.

hard.next_step: Demo scheduled with Farplas and EY team on 9th July

pulse.summary: Dark: no verified buyer engagement on record.

deltas.subject: EY's role and influence undefined: NextStep mentions 'Demo scheduled with Farplas and EY team on 9th July' but no field or call explains whether EY is an advisor, SI, implementation partner, or evaluation facilitator, and whether EY has a preferred vendor shortlist. deltas.subject: Competitive landscape unknown: no competitor documented in Salesforce or Avoma, no insight into who else Farplas is evaluating or what the current-state solution is. deltas.to: No quantified business case: no documented pain, metrics, or ROI. Without a quantified value case anchored to a pain owner, the deal cannot progress to Economic
deltas.subject: No quantified business case: no documented pain, metrics, or ROI. Without a quantified value case anchored to a pain owner, the deal cannot progress to Economic Buyer sponsorship or proposal. Discovery at the 9 July demo must extract the business problem, the operational or financial cost of the current state, and the quantified outcome Farplas is targeting.

Run deep discovery on the 9 July 2026 demo: surface the operational/financial pain Farplas is solving (procurement cycle time, contract compliance, sourcing inefficiency), quantify current-state impact (spend under management, manual process cost, supplier base size), map the competitive shortlist (incumbent, other vendors being evaluated), identify who owns the pain and who will approve budget (Economic Buyer), and ask how Farplas will run the evaluation (RFI, RFP, shortlist, PoC gates). This single call can close all the MEDDPICC knowledge gaps and determine whether the deal is winnable.

Multi-thread immediately: 0 buyer-side contact roles on this opp despite 19 account contacts spanning procurement, digital transformation, IT, and finance. Use the 9 July 2026 demo to identify at least 3 additional stakeholders (Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, pain owner) and add them to the opportunity as contact roles within 7 days. A single-thread deal is a ghost risk at any stage; get coverage now.

Clarify EY's role on the 9 July 2026 call: the Next Step says 'Demo scheduled with Farplas and EY team.' EY could be a consulting partner steering the evaluation, a systems integrator scoping implementation, or merely an observer. If EY is driving the process, understand their mandate, timeline, and how they will recommend vendors. If EY is an SI, confirm whether Farplas has already selected them or is still choosing. This shapes the decision process and partner strategy.
